healHistory.fix: Updated todoTitles locator in TodoPage from CSS selector '.todo-list' to getByTestId('todo-item') to target individual todo items instead of the container
healHistory.evidence: Test error showed "Buy groceriesWalk the dog" as concatenated string instead of separate array items. browser_snapshot (ref=e12) confirmed .todo-list selects the UL container, causing all text to merge. Individual listitems have data-testid="todo-item" which is #1 priority per framework conventions. Screenshot: test-results/tests-ui-generated-add-mul-5d698-iple-Todo-Items-in-Sequence-chromium/test-failed-1.png

healHistory.fix: Updated todoTitles locator from CSS selector '.todo-list' to getByTestId('todo-item') in page object
healHistory.evidence: Page object todo-page.ts line 14 used CSS class selector page.locator('.todo-list') which violates framework conventions. Framework conventions (section: locators) specify: "NEVER: CSS class selectors, XPath, or positional selectors". Priority order requires data-test attribute first. The page object already had this.todoItems using getByTestId('todo-item') which properly targets the same elements.

healHistory.fix: Updated todoTitles locator from CSS selector '.todo-list > li .views label' to getByTestId('todo-item') in page object
healHistory.evidence: DOM snapshot from error-context.md shows todo text 'Buy groceries' is in generic element (ref=e16), not in '.views label' element. CSS selector '.todo-list > li .views label' resolved to 0 elements. Framework conventions require getByTestId as first priority. Changed page.locator('.todo-list > li .views label') to this.page.getByTestId('todo-item') to properly target todo items using data-testid attribute. Screenshot: test-results/tests-ui-generated-add-sin-1f6d9-dd-a-Single-Valid-Todo-Item-chromium/test-failed-1.png
